title: Abrasive tools and methods for forming same
abstract: An abrasive tool having a bonded abrasive including a body having abrasive particles contained within a bond material and a barrier layer overlying a portion of the body and configured for removal by hand prior to use.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An abrasive tool comprising: a bonded abrasive including a body comprising abrasive particles contained within a bond material; and a barrier layer overlying a portion of the body and adhered to a major surface of the body in a peelable configuration having a peel strength of not greater than 3000 g/in. 2. The abrasive tool of claim 1, wherein the barrier layer is configured for removal by hand prior to use. 3. The abrasive tool of claim 1, further comprising an adhesive layer disposed between the major surface and the barrier layer, wherein the barrier layer is bonded to the major surface by the adhesive layer. 4. The abrasive tool of claim 3, wherein the adhesive layer comprises a pressure-sensitive adhesive. 5. The abrasive tool of claim 3, wherein the adhesive layer comprises a silicone based material. 6. The abrasive tool of claim 5, wherein the adhesive layer forms a waterproof bond between the barrier layer and the major surface. 7. The abrasive tool of claim 1, wherein the barrier layer comprises a metal foil. 8. The abrasive tool of claim 1, wherein the barrier layer comprises a polymer-containing film. 9. The abrasive tool of claim 1, wherein the barrier layer comprises a metalized film, an oxide-coated film, or a combination thereof. 10. The abrasive tool of claim 1, wherein the barrier layer comprises from an inner surface to an outer surface, a first tie layer, a metal-containing film, a second tie layer, and a polymer- containing film. 11. The abrasive tool of claim 10, wherein the polymer-containing film comprises polyester. 12. The abrasive tool of claim 10, wherein the barrier layer further comprises a sealant layer. 13. The abrasive tool of claim 1, wherein the major surface is a first major surface, and wherein the body comprises a second major surface opposite the first major surface and a peripheral edge extending between the first and second major surfaces, and wherein the barrier layer extends beyond the peripheral edge of the body. 14. The abrasive tool of claim 13, further comprising another barrier layer bonded to the second major surface of the body, wherein the barrier layers are attached to one another enclosing the body and forming a seal rim. 15. The abrasive tool of claim 1, further comprising a tab extending from the barrier layer in a sealed position and configured to be moved from the sealed position to an unsealed position and release at least a portion of the barrier layer from the portion of the body.
